name: Thymogen
subtitle: EW Dipeptide | Thymus Immune Bioregulator
overview: Thymogen (EW dipeptide) is a Khavinson bioregulator consisting of glutamic acid and tryptophan, originally isolated from calf thymus extracts (Thymalin) in the late 1980s. Developed by Professor Vladimir Khavinson, it has been registered in Russia since 1990 in multiple forms including injectable solution, nasal spray, and topical cream. Thymogen modulates both humoral and cellular immunity, activates T-cell differentiation, and has demonstrated geroprotective (anti-aging) and antitumor activities in research studies.
mechanism: Thymogen works through multiple immunomodulatory mechanisms: (1) activates T-cell differentiation and T-cell recognition of peptide-MHC complexes, (2) induces changes in intracellular cyclic nucleotide composition, (3) activates neutrophilic chemotaxis and phagocytosis, (4) normalizes T-lymphocyte concentrations and ratios (CD3+, CD4+, CD8+), (5) stimulates production of immunoglobulins (IgA, IgG, IgE, IgM), and (6) enhances lymphocyte differentiation receptor expression. Research suggests Thymogen may interact specifically with the AACG DNA sequence, affecting gene expression. The peptide is rapidly distributed to thymus, lymph nodes, liver, adrenals, and kidneys.

items.name: Immune System Modulation
items.description: Modulates both humoral and cellular immune responses for balanced immunity.

items.name: T-Cell Function
items.description: Activates T-cell differentiation and normalizes CD3+, CD4+, CD8+ populations.

items.name: Immunoglobulin Production
items.description: Stimulates production of IgA, IgG, IgE, and IgM antibodies.

items.name: Post-Operative Recovery
items.description: Studied for improving surgical outcomes through immune support.

items.name: Geroprotection
items.description: Demonstrated geroprotector activity in aging research.

items.name: Lifespan Extension
items.description: In combination with pineal peptides, showed significant mean lifespan increases in animal models.

items.name: Antitumor Activity
items.description: Inhibits spontaneous carcinogenesis and shows robust antitumor effects in research.

items.name: Cancer Support
items.description: Studied in Russia for regulating immune response in oncology patients.

items.name: Diabetes Support
items.description: Investigated for metabolic and immune aspects of diabetes.

deliveryMethods.overview: Injectable form for intramuscular or subcutaneous administration. This is the original delivery method registered in Russia. Typical protocols involve 10-20 day cycles repeated 2-3 times per year.
Metered-dose nasal spray form registered in Russia. Convenient for non-injection administration with good absorption through nasal mucosa.
0.05% cream formulation registered in Russia for external use. Applied to skin for local effects.

interactions.notes: Combination showed significant lifespan increases and robust antitumor activity in research.
Thymogen was isolated from Thymalin; they share complementary thymus-supporting mechanisms.
Both are Khavinson thymus dipeptides; often combined in comprehensive immune protocols.
Different thymic peptides with complementary immune mechanisms.
Different structures and mechanisms; can be used together.

timeline.effects: Rapid tissue distribution to thymus, lymph nodes, liver, kidneys
Immunoglobulin production stimulated; immune markers improve
Effects persist due to cellular and epigenetic changes
Cumulative geroprotective benefits with regular cycling

common: Minimal side effects reported in clinical use
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to component amino acids

good: Certificate of analysis with purity verification

references.title: Immunomodulatory synthetic dipeptide L-Glu-L-Trp slows down aging and inhibits spontaneous carcinogenesis in rats
references.keyFindings: Thymogen demonstrated geroprotector activity and inhibited spontaneous carcinogenesis in aging rat models.

references.title: Natural and synthetic thymic peptides as therapeutics for immune dysfunction
references.keyFindings: Review of thymic peptide therapeutics including Thymogen for immune modulation.

references.title: Peptide Bioregulators from Thymus and Pineal Gland
references.keyFindings: Significant lifespan increases and antitumor activity with combined Thymogen and Epitalon treatment.

references.title: Thymogen: Mechanism of Action Studies
references.keyFindings: Thymogen activates T-cell differentiation, modulates cyclic nucleotides, and may interact with AACG DNA sequences.

quickStats.storage: Injectable: 2-8°C. Nasal/topical: per product directions. Powder: room temp
